action_set_definitions.name: get_active_contract_metadata
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the metadata for the contract currently under review, including the unique contract ID and current status. Essential for initiating the workflow.

action_set_definitions.name: get_contract_text
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the full raw text of the contract associated with the provided contract_id.

contract_id.description: The unique identifier of the contract.

action_set_definitions.name: log_contract_issue
action_set_definitions.description: Records a specific legal or operational issue found during the review process into the tracked issues list.

contract_id.description: The unique identifier of the contract.

issue_description.description: A detailed description of the identified non-compliance or risk.

action_set_definitions.name: update_contract_content
action_set_definitions.description: Updates the full text of the contract with redlined/revised content. Use this to implement changes to clauses.

contract_id.description: The unique identifier of the contract.

revised_text.description: The full document text incorporating all necessary redlines and revisions.

action_set_definitions.name: update_review_status
action_set_definitions.description: Updates the workflow phase/status of the contract (e.g., transitioning to 'REDLINING').

contract_id.description: The unique identifier of the contract.

state_description.contract_id: Unique identifier for the NDA document.
state_description.document_text: The complete raw text of the NDA contract currently being reviewed.
state_description.review_status: The current phase of the workflow (e.g., 'INITIAL_SCREENING', 'REDLINING', 'PENDING_APPROVAL', 'FINALIZED').
state_description.identified_issues: A list of tracked legal issues or risks identified in the contract.
state_description.parties: The legal entities involved in the agreement.
state_description.last_action_timestamp: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last state update.

task_initial_state.document_text: Section 6. Data Remediation: Upon the Disclosing Party's written request, the Receiving Party shall within 48 hours expunge all Confidential Information from its active servers and all disaster recovery backups. No exceptions shall be made for legal holds or regulatory archiving. Following the 48-hour period, a signed Certificate of Destruction must be delivered to the Disclosing Party within 24 hours, certifying that no copies remain. Any violation of this timeline constitutes a material breach.
